paragraph: Single-family homes in Ridgefield are selling at a median of $735,000 with a sale-to-list ratio right at 1.0 and about 40 percent of homes closing above asking. Median days on market sit at 26 days, which puts this in competitive but balanced territory. Homes priced right are moving, but there's no bidding war dynamic here. The range is wide, from $500,000 to $1.51 million, so your mileage depends heavily on what you're looking for and where in town it sits. Multi-family properties tell a different story. With 11 sales at a median of $999,999, they're moving faster at a median of 16 days on market, and 64 percent are selling above asking. The sale-to-list ratio hits 1.05, which signals real momentum. If you're considering a rental property or multi-unit investment, this tier is where buyers are seeing competition. Condos and townhouses round out the market at a much lower price point, with a median of $322,500 and 31 days on market. That category moved six homes over eight months, so supply is thin and movement is slower. If you're weighing single-family against multi-family, the deciding factor isn't market heat. It's whether you want the space and grounds of a single-family home or the income potential and seller momentum of a multi-unit property.
